extra.message: Untrusted HTTP input flows into an LDAP search filter. Concatenating request-controlled data into the filter of an `ldap.NewSearchRequest` / `ldap.SearchRequest` allows an attacker to alter the filter structure (LDAP injection) and read or bypass directory records. Escape the untrusted value with `ldap.EscapeFilter` before building the filter.

cwe: CWE-90: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an LDAP Query ('LDAP Injection')

extra.message: Untrusted HTTP input flows into an LDAP search filter. Concatenating request-controlled data into the `filter` option of an LDAP search allows an attacker to alter the filter structure (LDAP injection) and read or bypass directory records. Escape the untrusted value (e.g. the `ldap-escape` package) before building the filter.

cwe: CWE-90: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an LDAP Query ('LDAP Injection')

extra.message: Untrusted HTTP input flows into an LDAP search filter. Concatenating request-controlled data into an LDAP filter allows an attacker to alter the filter structure (LDAP injection) and read or bypass directory records. Escape the untrusted value with `ldap.filter.escape_filter_chars` (python-ldap) or `ldap3.utils.conv.escape_filter_chars` before building the filter.

cwe: CWE-90: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an LDAP Query ('LDAP Injection')

extra.message: Untrusted HTTP input flows into an XPath expression. Concatenating request-controlled data into an XPath query allows an attacker to alter the query structure (XPath injection), reading arbitrary nodes from the XML document. Build the query with a precompiled/parameterized expression instead of concatenating the untrusted value into the path string.

cwe: CWE-643: Improper Neutralization of Data within XPath Expressions ('XPath Injection')

extra.message: Untrusted HTTP input flows into an XPath expression. Concatenating request-controlled data into an XPath query allows an attacker to alter the query structure (XPath injection), reading arbitrary nodes from the XML document. Build the query with a parameterized/precompiled expression instead of concatenating the untrusted value into the path string.

cwe: CWE-643: Improper Neutralization of Data within XPath Expressions ('XPath Injection')

extra.message: Untrusted HTTP input flows into an XPath expression. Concatenating request-controlled data into an XPath query allows an attacker to alter the query structure (XPath injection), reading arbitrary nodes from the XML document. Use a parameterized XPath instead — build the query with variable placeholders (e.g. lxml `etree.XPath("...=$v", ...)` bound via `variables=` or a call-time keyword) so the untrusted value is data, never query syntax.

cwe: CWE-643: Improper Neutralization of Data within XPath Expressions ('XPath Injection')

extra.message: Untrusted HTTP input flows into an XPath expression. Concatenating request-controlled data into an XPath query allows an attacker to alter the query structure (XPath injection), reading arbitrary nodes from the XML document. Use a parameterized XPath instead — build the query with variable placeholders (e.g. lxml `etree.XPath("...=$v", ...)` bound via `variables=` or a call-time keyword) so the untrusted value is data, never query syntax.

cwe: CWE-643: Improper Neutralization of Data within XPath Expressions ('XPath Injection')
